Today is February 14, 2026. Regarding the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie in Tucson, what specific detail did the FBI provide about the backpack the suspect was seen wearing on doorbell camera footage?
(A) It was a 25-litre Ozark Trail Hiker Pack
(B) It was a 30-litre Ozark Trail Hiker Pack
(C) It was a 25-litre North Face Trail Hiker Pack
(D) It was a black 25-litre backpack with no visible branding
(E) It was a 20-litre Ozark Trail Hiker Pack
explanation: The article states that the FBI analyzed doorbell camera footage and determined the suspect was wearing a "25-litre Ozark Trail Hiker Pack backpack."
model_output:
Based on web searches and browsing specific articles, including the official FBI website, the details provided about the backpack consistently describe it as a black 25-liter Ozark Trail Hiker Pack. One source had a minor discrepancy (listing it as 24-liter), but this appears to be a typo, as multiple other sources—including the FBI's own page—confirm 25 liters. This matches option (A).[[1]](https://www.fbi.gov/wanted/kidnap/nancy-guthrie/@@download.pdf)[[2]](https://www.fox10phoenix.com/news/nancy-guthrie-fbi-identifies-ozark-trail-backpack-key-clue-search)[[3]](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/nancy-guthrie-case-fbi-releases-first-description-of-suspect-increases-reward-to-100000-dollars)

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- FBI.gov ([https://www.fbi.gov/wanted/kidnap/nancy-guthrie/@@download.pdf])
- FOX10 Phoenix ([https://www.fox10phoenix.com/news/nancy-guthrie-fbi-identifies-ozark-trail-backpack-key-clue-search])
- CBS News ([https://www.cbsnews.com/news/nancy-guthrie-case-fbi-releases-first-description-of-suspect-increases-reward-to-100000-dollars])

Today is February 14, 2026. In the aftermath of the mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, the RCMP identified the suspect's mother as one of the victims. What was her name?
(A) Shannda Aviugana-Durand
(B) Jennifer Jacobs
(C) Colaina Hall
(D) Sarah Lampert
(E) Mary Simon
explanation: The article identifies the suspect's mother as "Jennifer Jacobs, who was also known as Jennifer Strang."
model_output:
The mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, BC, occurred on February 10, 2026. The suspect was identified as 18-year-old Jesse Van Rootselaar. Multiple sources confirm that his mother was among the victims killed at the family residence prior to the school attack. She is named as Jennifer Jacobs (also sometimes referred to as Jennifer Strang, possibly a married or former name), and this matches how she was identified in official reports.[[1]](https://globalnews.ca/news/11665121/tumbler-ridge-bc-mass-shooting-victims-school)[[2]](https://www.cbc.ca/news/tumbler-ridge-mass-shooting-victims-9.7086903)

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

- [CBC News] (https://www.cbc.ca/news/tumbler-ridge-mass-shooting-victims-9.7086903)

Today is February 14, 2026. During the partial government shutdown affecting the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), what specific reform have Democrats been demanding regarding the appearance of immigration agents?
(A) A requirement for agents to wear body cameras at all times
(B) A mandate for agents to wear high-visibility vests during raids
(C) A requirement for agents to display their full names on their uniforms
(D) A ban on immigration agents wearing face masks
(E) A restriction on agents using unmarked tactical gear
explanation: The article mentions that Democrats have asked for several changes, including "a ban on immigration agents wearing face masks."
model_output:
The sources indicate that Democrats have a list of 10 demands for ICE/DHS reforms as a condition for ending the partial shutdown. Several relate to agents' appearance (e.g., bans on masks, requirements to display ID including full names, body cameras, and restrictions/standardization on tactical gear and uniforms). However, the specific reform most prominently tied to agents' appearance—and the one repeatedly highlighted as a key sticking point in negotiations—is a ban on immigration agents wearing face masks during operations, as this has been described as creating a "secret police" appearance and eroding trust.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [The Guardian] (https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/05/democrats-ice-reforms-funding-bill)[[1]](https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/05/democrats-ice-reforms-funding-bill)
- [Los Angeles Times] (https://www.latimes.com/politics/story/2026-02-13/disputes-over-immigration-enforcement-tactics-trigger-partial-government-shutdown)[[2]](https://www.latimes.com/politics/story/2026-02-13/disputes-over-immigration-enforcement-tactics-trigger-partial-government-shutdown)

- [Reuters] (https://www.reuters.com/world/us/us-homeland-security-department-heading-into-partial-shutdown-saturday-2026-02-13)[[4]](https://www.reuters.com/world/us/us-homeland-security-department-heading-into-partial-shutdown-saturday-2026-02-13)
- [CBS News] (https://www.cbsnews.com/news/homeland-security-funding-immigration-reforms-senate-democrats-vote)[[5]](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/homeland-security-funding-immigration-reforms-senate-democrats-vote)

(A) A humanitarian mission to Africa
(B) A business meeting regarding the Los Angeles Olympics
(C) A private vacation to the Caribbean
(D) A fundraising event for the Clinton Foundation in New York
(E) A talent scouting trip for his agency
explanation: The LA28 board statement said Wasserman and his wife flew on "a humanitarian mission to Africa on Epstein's plane at the invitation of the Clinton Foundation in 2003."
model_output:
The information comes from the LA28 board's official statement, as reported in recent news articles following the review of Epstein-related documents. The board explicitly described the 2003 flight as a humanitarian mission to Africa, at the invitation of the Clinton Foundation (accompanied by Wasserman's then-wife), and noted it as his only interaction with Epstein.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

Today is February 14, 2026. Mikhail Shaidorov won gold in men's figure skating at the Milan-Cortina Olympics. This was the first Winter Olympic gold for Kazakhstan since which year?
(A) 1976
(B) 1984
(C) 1994
(D) 2004
(E) 2014
explanation: The article states: "This is Kazakhstan's first Winter Olympic gold medal since Lillehammer 1994, when Vladimir Smirnov won the men's 50km cross-country skiing."
model_output:
Based on historical records, Kazakhstan has competed independently in the Winter Olympics since 1994, and their only gold medal prior to the 2026 win by Mikhail Shaidorov was in 1994 (won by Vladimir Smirnov in cross-country skiing). No Winter Olympic golds were won by the country in the intervening years.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

model_output:
Based on recent reports, Nikhil Gupta pleaded guilty to charges including murder-for-hire, conspiracy to commit murder-for-hire, and conspiracy to commit money laundering in connection with the plot to assassinate Gurpatwant Singh Pannun in New York[[1]](https://www.ndtv.com/world-news/indian-man-nikhil-gupta-pleads-guilty-in-foiled-murder-plot-against-khalistani-terrorist-gurpatwant-singh-pannun-in-new-york-11001862). These charges carry a combined maximum sentence of 40 years in prison[[2]](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/13/nyregion/nikhil-gupta-assasination-india-sikh-plea.html)[[1]](https://www.ndtv.com/world-news/indian-man-nikhil-gupta-pleads-guilty-in-foiled-murder-plot-against-khalistani-terrorist-gurpatwant-singh-pannun-in-new-york-11001862).

Today is February 14, 2026. US inflation cooled to 2.4% in the 12 months to January. According to the Department of Labor, what was the inflation rate reported for the prior month (December)?
(A) 2.0%
(B) 2.7%
(C) 2.9%
(D) 3.1%
(E) 2.4%
explanation: The article states: "The consumer price index rose by 2.4% over the 12 months to January... That was down from 2.7% in the prior month."
model_output:
The US inflation rate (CPI) cooled to 2.4% year-over-year for the 12 months ending in January 2026, as stated in the query. The prior month's rate—for the 12 months ending in December 2025—was reported as 2.7% by the Department of Labor's Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS).[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/inflation-slowed-in-january-as-consumer-prices-rise-24-over-prior-year-to-start-2026-133531279.html)[[2]](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/13/business/inflation-cpi-report-january.html)[[3]](https://tradingeconomics.com/united-states/inflation-cpi)[[4]](https://www.nysut.org/resources/all-listing/2026/january/consumer-price-index-december-2025)[[5]](https://www.bls.gov/opub/ted/2026/consumer-price-index-2025-in-review.htm)

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

(B) $1,760
(C) $7,500
(D) $2,400
(E) $18,000
explanation: The article states: "Reversing the finding will reduce automobile manufacturers' costs by around $2,400 (£1,760) per car, the White House claimed."
model_output:
Based on recent news from February 2026, the Trump administration's reversal of the 2009 EPA endangerment finding is claimed to reduce costs for automobile manufacturers. Specifically, White House statements indicate a per-vehicle cost reduction of $2,400[[1]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cn0zdd7yl4vo).

<final_answer>
(D)

Today is February 14, 2026. Despite a growing economy, the US labor market has seen a slowdown. According to the report, what was the average number of jobs added per month in the US last year?
(A) 150,000
(B) 44,000
(C) 1,200
(D) 2,000
(E) 15,000
explanation: The article states: "Last year the US added an average of just 15,000 jobs a month, very few by historic standards."
model_output:
Based on the query, which references February 14, 2026, as the current date, "last year" refers to 2025. The phrasing points to the most recent U.S. jobs report at that time (the BLS Employment Situation report for January 2026, released around February 11, 2026), which included significant downward revisions to the 2025 job growth data. Multiple sources confirm that the revised total job additions for 2025 were 181,000, which averages out to 15,000 jobs per month[[1]](https://groundworkcollaborative.org/news/revised-data-paints-a-grim-picture-of-2025-jobs-market)[[2]](https://www.statista.com/chart/35002/us-job-growth-and-unemployment-rate?srsltid=AfmBOoo_3PWzJtqW0x5Xs3wc_vgmC5BWl-giYdFBVV4f5RURplVQF58o)[[3]](https://www.bls.gov/news.release/pdf/empsit.pdf). This fits the description of a slowdown in the labor market despite broader economic growth.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- NBC News (https://www.nbcnews.com/business/economy/january-jobs-revisions-trump-rcna258398)
- Groundwork Collaborative (https://groundworkcollaborative.org/news/revised-data-paints-a-grim-picture-of-2025-jobs-market)
- BLS.gov (https://www.bls.gov/news.release/pdf/empsit.pdf)
- BBC (https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c5y5xjzpr52o)
- Reuters (https://www.reuters.com/business/us-job-growth-accelerates-january-unemployment-rate-falls-43-2026-02-11)
- Statista (https://www.statista.com/chart/35002/us-job-growth-and-unemployment-rate?srsltid=AfmBOoo_3PWzJtqW0x5Xs3wc_vgmC5BWl-giYdFBVV4f5RURplVQF58o)
- Eye on Housing (https://eyeonhousing.org/2026/02/job-growth-starts-year-on-strong-note-however-2025-revisions-offer-caution)

model_output:

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

(B) Weyes Blood
(C) Abby Wambach
(D) Wednesday
(E) Orville Peck
explanation: The article lists several artists leaving, including "country music singer-songwriter Orville Peck."
model_output:
Based on recent news reports from February 2026, several artists have indeed left or announced plans to leave Casey Wasserman's talent agency following disclosures of his past communications and links to Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell. Among the options, the one specifically identified as a country music singer-songwriter who announced an exit is Orville Peck[[1]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cy0dn4e1njjo)[[2]](https://www.tennessean.com/story/entertainment/music/2026/02/10/orville-peck-exit-wasserman-epstein-files/88610590007)[[3]](https://www.rollingstone.com/music/music-news/weyes-blood-chappell-roan-leave-wasserman-agency-epstein-1235514583). The other options are either not country musicians (e.g., Chappell Roan is pop/indie, Weyes Blood is indie/folk, Wednesday is a band in the indie/shoegaze space) or not musicians at all (Abby Wambach is a retired soccer player).

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is February 14, 2026. Ilia Malinin landed a backflip at the Milan-Cortina Games, a move that was previously banned. Who was the US champion who last landed a legal backflip at the Olympics in 1976 before the ban was implemented?
(A) Surya Bonaly
(B) Terry Kubicka
(C) Mikhail Shaidorov
(D) Yuma Kagiyama
(E) Shun Sato
explanation: The article states: "In the team event he had also landed the first legal backflip at an Olympic Games since US champion Terry Kubicka in 1976 - after which it was banned for safety reasons."
model_output:
The question asks for the US champion who performed a legal backflip at the 1976 Winter Olympics before the move was banned by the International Skating Union. Based on web search results, this was American figure skater Terry Kubicka, who landed the backflip during the men's free skate event at the Innsbruck Games on February 11, 1976[[1]](https://www.forbes.com/sites/carolineprice/2026/02/09/the-complicated-and-controversial-history-of-figure-skatings-backflip). He was also the 1976 US men's champion[[2]](https://usfigureskating.org/news/2024/10/3/rinkside-the-backflip-is-well-back.aspx). The backflip was banned shortly after his performance due to safety concerns[[3]](https://www.bet.com/article/y2rp54/from-deductions-to-gold-the-backflip-that-figure-skating-finally-decided-to-celebrate).

None of the other options fit: Surya Bonaly is French and performed an illegal backflip in 1998; Mikhail Shaidorov is a Kazakhstani skater; and Yuma Kagiyama and Shun Sato are both Japanese skaters from the modern era.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- NPR ([https://www.npr.org/2026/02/09/nx-s1-5705380/figure-skating-backflip-malinin])
- Forbes ([https://www.forbes.com/sites/carolineprice/2026/02/09/the-complicated-and-controversial-history-of-figure-skatings-backflip])
- Britannica ([https://www.britannica.com/question/Who-did-the-first-backflip-in-figure-skating])
- U.S. Figure Skating ([https://usfigureskating.org/news/2024/10/3/rinkside-the-backflip-is-well-back.aspx])

Today is February 14, 2026. In the Nikhil Gupta murder-for-hire case, which Indian government department housed the intelligence service (Research and Analysis Wing) where the officer Vikash Yadav allegedly worked?
(A) Ministry of External Affairs
(B) Department of Justice
(C) Drug Enforcement Administration
(D) Cabinet Secretariat
(E) Ministry of Home Affairs
explanation: The article states: "The officer, Vikash Yadav, worked for Indian government's Cabinet Secretariat, which houses India's foreign intelligence service, the Research and Analysis Wing..."
model_output:
The Nikhil Gupta case involves an alleged plot to assassinate a Sikh activist in the US, with Gupta charged in connection to it. Reports indicate that Vikash Yadav was an officer in India's Research and Analysis Wing (RAW), which is the country's foreign intelligence agency. Based on official structures, RAW operates under the Cabinet Secretariat and reports directly to the Prime Minister of India (not under a specific ministry like External Affairs or Home Affairs).

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Indian_intelligence_agencies)[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Indian_intelligence_agencies)
